key_claim: Treating synthetic data differently from real data during training can prevent model autophagy disorder and enable self-improving diffusion models.
methodology: Proposed SIMS training concept that distinguishes synthetic from real data in diffusion model training loops
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether physically-verified authentic human behavioral data provides qualitatively different training signals compared to curated real data.
theoretical_framework: Self-IMproving diffusion models with Synthetic data (SIMS)

key_claim: Introducing an idealized correction function that maps data points toward the true distribution makes self-consuming loops exponentially more stable.
methodology: Theoretical analysis of self-consuming loops with correction functions, plus proposed self-correction functions relying on expert knowledge
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider that the true data distribution itself may be degrading due to web-scale synthetic contamination, undermining the correction target.
theoretical_framework: Self-correcting self-consuming loop framework with idealized correction functions

key_claim: Indiscriminate use of model-generated content in training causes irreversible defects where tails of the original content distribution disappear, termed model collapse.
methodology: Theoretical and empirical analysis of GPT-generation language models trained recursively on their own outputs
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether authentic human social behavioral data could serve as a robust anchor to prevent tail collapse in recursive training.
theoretical_framework: Model collapse framework describing tail disappearance in iteratively retrained generative models

key_claim: Self-consuming generative models with human-curated data provably converge toward distributions that optimize human preferences rather than collapsing.
methodology: Theoretical analysis of iterative retraining with curated synthetic data under human feedback selection
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether human curation preferences themselves degrade diversity by filtering out tail distributions of authentic human behavior.
theoretical_framework: Iterative retraining framework with preference-based data curation proving convergence to preference-optimal distributions

key_claim: Properties of the original human training data predictably modulate the magnitude of distribution shifts caused by recursive training loops in LLMs.
methodology: Exhaustive manipulation of human dataset properties combined with regression analyses across recursive LLM training loops
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether physically-verified authentic human social behavioral data exhibits properties that are inherently more resistant to recursive distribution shift.

key_claim: Adversarial or noisy data curation in self-consuming loops can destabilize convergence guarantees and enable malicious manipulation of model distributions.
methodology: Theoretical analysis extending self-consuming loop models to adversarial and noisy curation settings
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider whether grounding training data in verified physical human behavioral signals could provide robustness against adversarial curation attacks.
theoretical_framework: Adversarially curated self-consuming generative model framework extending Ferbach et al. (2024)

key_claim: Reversing the direction of self-training degradation via negative extrapolation transforms model collapse signals into a mechanism for self-improvement in image generation.
methodology: Fine-tuning a base model on self-synthesized data then reversing the update direction (negative extrapolation) to improve sample quality and diversity
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether negative extrapolation can recover lost tail distributions representing rare but authentic human behavioral patterns.

key_claim: Model collapse can be characterized and mitigated directly from next-token probability distributions without requiring knowledge of whether training data is human- or AI-generated.
methodology: Introduction of surplexity-based measures derived from next-token probability distributions to detect and mitigate model collapse
limitation: Existing mitigation methods assume reliable knowledge of whether training data is human-authored or AI-generated, which is often unavailable.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not explore whether physically-verified human behavioral data provides entropy or diversity signals that are fundamentally distinct from web-scraped text.
theoretical_framework: Surplexity framework for characterizing collapse via next-token probability distributions

key_claim: Self-consuming training loops in LLMs reduce both quality and diversity of generated text, analogous to model collapse observed in image generation.
methodology: Empirical study of iterative LLM retraining on own outputs with a novel logic-expression-based method for verifying correctness of generated content
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether incorporating physically-verified authentic human behavioral data could prevent or reverse the observed collapse in LLMs.

key_claim: Recursive stability of self-consuming training loops depends on both model architecture and the proportion of real versus synthetic data.
methodology: Theoretical generalization analysis introducing the notion of recursive stability, extended to transformer architectures
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider whether the quality or provenance of real data (e.g., physically-verified human behavioral data vs. web-scraped data) affects recursive stability.
theoretical_framework: Recursive stability framework for self-consuming training loops

key_claim: CFG scale is the key factor driving model collapse in chains of diffusion finetuning on self-generated images.
methodology: Empirical analysis of iterative diffusion model finetuning combined with theoretical analysis based on quantitative trait modeling from statistical genetics
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether grounding training data in authentic human-generated content rather than synthetic outputs could mitigate collapse without architectural fixes.
theoretical_framework: Quantitative trait modeling from statistical genetics applied to diffusion model collapse

key_claim: Model collapse is unavoidable when training solely on synthetic data, but mixing real and synthetic data allows estimation of a maximal safe proportion of synthetic data.
methodology: Statistical modeling to characterize impact of recursive training scenarios on language model distributions, with analysis of tail disappearance
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not distinguish between different qualities of real data (e.g., web-scraped vs. physically-verified human behavioral data) when analyzing collapse mitigation.
theoretical_framework: Statistical model for recursive training characterizing tail disappearance in language model distributions

key_claim: Low-dimensional latent representations of synthetic data degrade over self-consuming generations, and filtering less realistic synthetic data in latent space mitigates model collapse.
methodology: Empirical analysis of latent space dynamics across self-consuming diffusion model generations, proposing Latent Space Filtering (LSF) to remove unrealistic synthetic samples
limitation: Common strategies like accumulating historical data or injecting fresh real data either increase computational cost or require expensive human annotation.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not evaluate whether authentic human social behavioral data could serve as a more efficient or effective anchor than generic real data for preventing collapse.

key_claim: Spectral analysis of embedding Gram matrices provides mathematically grounded metrics to quantify and predict the onset of model collapse in LLMs.
methodology: Derivation of deterministic and stochastic bounds on the spectrum of embedding Gram matrices to track distributional contraction
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether spectral signatures differ between models trained on web-scraped data versus physically-verified human behavioral data.
theoretical_framework: SIGMA (Spectral Inequalities for Gram Matrix Analysis)

key_claim: Accumulating data across generations prevents complete model collapse in discrete distribution estimation under self-consuming loops, with established minimax bounds.
methodology: Minimax analysis of recursive discrete distribution estimation under data accumulation in self-consuming settings
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether the source authenticity of accumulated real data (web-scraped vs. verified human data) affects the tightness of non-collapse guarantees.
theoretical_framework: Minimax bounds framework for recursive discrete distribution estimation

key_claim: Co-evolving generative models that shape each other's training through iterative feedback exhibit distinct convergence dynamics from single self-consuming models.
methodology: Theoretical analysis modeling text as multinomial and image as conditional multi-dimensional Gaussian in a co-evolving multimodal system
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider whether injecting physically-verified human behavioral signals into the co-evolution loop could stabilize cross-modal feedback dynamics.
theoretical_framework: Co-evolving generative model framework with multinomial-Gaussian coupled dynamics

key_claim: Model collapse in diffusion models manifests as a transition from generalization to memorization, driven by declining entropy of synthetic training data across iterations.
methodology: Iterative training of diffusion models on their own synthetic outputs, measuring entropy decline and memorization metrics across generations
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether incorporating physically-verified authentic human behavioral data could halt or reverse the entropy decline driving model collapse.
theoretical_framework: Generalization-to-memorization transition framework linked to synthetic data entropy decline

key_claim: Verification of synthesized data can prevent model collapse, as it is easier for humans and machines to distinguish good from bad examples than to generate high-quality samples.
methodology: Theoretical characterization using Gaussian mixtures, linear classifiers, and linear verifiers to derive measurable conditions for effective verification
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether verification can compensate for the loss of rare, tail-distributed authentic human behavioral signals in web-scraped corpora.
theoretical_framework: Gaussian mixture model with linear classifiers and linear verifiers for analyzing synthesized data verification

key_claim: Without enough fresh real data in each generation of an autophagous loop, future generative models are doomed to have their precision or diversity progressively decrease.
methodology: Empirical analysis of three autophagous loop families using state-of-the-art generative image models, varying fresh real data availability and quality-diversity trade-offs
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether specific types of real data (e.g., physically-verified human social behavioral data) are more effective than generic real data at preventing model autophagy.
theoretical_framework: Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD) framework characterizing autophagous generative loops

key_claim: Synthetic data contamination in training corpora causes a wide range of scaling law decay phenomena, including loss of scaling and tail narrowing across model generations.
methodology: Theoretical framework analyzing model collapse through the lens of neural scaling laws, characterizing decay phenomena as synthetic data enters training corpora
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the tail collapse predicted by modified scaling laws can be specifically counteracted by anchoring training on verified authentic human behavioral data.
theoretical_framework: Scaling law framework for model collapse analyzing loss of scaling, shifted scaling, and tail narrowing under synthetic data contamination

key_claim: Without sufficient fresh real data in each generation, self-consuming generative models are doomed to progressive quality or diversity degradation (Model Autophagy Disorder).
methodology: Analytical and empirical analysis of three autophagous loop families using state-of-the-art generative image models, varying data freshness and quality-diversity bias
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether physically-grounded human behavioral data offers qualitatively different protection against autophagous collapse compared to generic fresh real data.
theoretical_framework: Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD) framework for self-consuming generative loops

key_claim: Increasing the number of distinct language models in an AI ecosystem mitigates knowledge collapse that occurs when a single model is trained on its own outputs.
methodology: Segmenting training data across increasing numbers of language models and evaluating ecosystem performance over ten self-training iterations, inspired by ecological diversity principles
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether epistemic diversity among models can compensate for the fundamental loss of rare, tail-distributed human behavioral signals in degraded web-scraped training data.
theoretical_framework: Ecology-inspired epistemic diversity framework for AI model ecosystems

key_claim: GenAI follows a 'democratization-average trap-model collapse' trajectory where outputs progressively lose human sensibilities and individuality.
methodology: Conceptual framework development projecting GenAI trajectory and its implications for consumer research data and models
what_it_does_NOT_address: How physically-verified authentic consumer behavioral data (as opposed to GenAI-generated survey or text data) could serve as a corrective anchor against the average trap.
theoretical_framework: Democratization-average trap-model collapse trajectory framework for GenAI in consumer research

key_claim: The augment workflow, where original real data is retained alongside synthetic data across generations, universally avoids model collapse with a π²/6 pathway governing test error.
methodology: Mathematical analysis deriving universal constants governing model collapse avoidance under augment versus discard workflows, validated on canonical datasets
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the π²/6 pathway holds when the original real data itself suffers from information degradation such as tail collapse or entropy decline in web-scraped corpora.
theoretical_framework: π²/6 universality framework for scaling behavior of test error under augmented synthetic data training

key_claim: LLM output homogenization is best understood through the conceptual lens of Frankfurtian bullshit, extending beyond the hallucination-focused framing.
methodology: Philosophical conceptual analysis applying Frankfurt's bullshit framework to LLM output homogenization patterns
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not empirically measure the degree to which LLM output homogenization feeds back into training data to cause measurable entropy decline or diversity loss in web corpora.
theoretical_framework: Frankfurtian bullshit framework applied to LLM output homogenization

key_claim: Machine-generated text detection can be used to filter synthetic data from training sets and thereby prevent model collapse.
methodology: Analysis of decoding strategy impact on model collapse across iterative model generations, measuring text characteristics, similarity to human references, and model performance
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not evaluate whether detection-based filtering can preserve the tail distribution and diversity of authentic human behavioral signals in web-scraped corpora.

key_claim: Recursive training on mixtures of human and AI-generated data leads to model collapse, and this paper provides theoretical guarantees beyond simplified Gaussian or discrete settings.
methodology: Mathematical/theoretical analysis of contaminated recursive training dynamics under generalized data distribution assumptions
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether incorporating physically-verified authentic human behavioral data can serve as an anchor to prevent collapse in recursive training scenarios.
theoretical_framework: Theoretical framework for generative AI under contaminated recursive training with non-Gaussian/non-discrete data distributions

key_claim: Paraphrasing AI-generated text successfully evades multiple detectors including watermarking and GPTZero, but retrieval-based detection is an effective defense.
methodology: Building an 11B parameter paraphrase model (DIPPER) to stress-test AI text detectors across three LLMs, evaluating watermarking, GPTZero, DetectGPT, and OpenAI's classifier
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how paraphrased AI-generated text that evades detection accumulates in web-scraped training corpora, contributing to information degradation and tail collapse.

key_claim: AI-generated code can be detected using targeted masking perturbation paired with perplexity-based analysis of large language models.
methodology: Targeted masking perturbation combined with perplexity scoring from LLMs to distinguish AI-generated from human-written code assignments
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider how undetected AI-generated code entering public repositories could degrade the diversity and authenticity of code training data over time.

key_claim: Detection of synthetic content across modalities achieves varying accuracy, with independent stylometric analysis reaching approximately 80% accuracy.
methodology: Systematic review of synthetic content detection approaches across text, image, audio, and video modalities including stylometric analysis, watermarking, pixel prediction, and hybrid methods
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not assess how undetected synthetic content accumulates in web-scraped datasets and progressively erodes the information quality and diversity of training corpora.

key_claim: Current AI-generated text detection methods including watermarking, statistical analysis, and ML classification face significant challenges as LLM outputs become increasingly indistinguishable from human text.
methodology: Comprehensive survey of state-of-the-art AIGT detection approaches, datasets, and factors influencing detectability
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how failure to detect AI-generated text at scale leads to progressive contamination and entropy decline in web-scraped training corpora.

key_claim: Digital watermarking has evolved from copyright protection to applications including fake news detection, and a meta-survey of 64 selected surveys reveals the breadth and fragmentation of the field.
methodology: Meta-survey analyzing 64 selected survey papers on digital media watermarking, synthesizing techniques across images, video, audio, and speech
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not evaluate whether watermarking techniques can effectively prevent synthetic content from contaminating web-scraped training data and contributing to information degradation.

key_claim: LLM-generated text detectors are vulnerable to adversarial attacks using synonym replacement and instructional prompt-based style alteration powered by auxiliary LLMs.
methodology: Two adversarial attack strategies against LLM detectors: context-aware synonym replacement and automated instructional prompt search using an auxiliary LLM
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether degradation of web text quality through adversarial evasion contaminates future training corpora scraped from the web.

key_claim: RoBERTa-based classifiers show uncertain reliability in detecting AI-assisted plagiarism in ESL writing contexts.
methodology: Cross-disciplinary descriptive study evaluating two RoBERTa-based classifiers on a dataset of 240 human-written and ChatGPT-generated ESL texts
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider how AI-generated ESL text entering the web may degrade the diversity and authenticity of language data available for future model training.

key_claim: AI has introduced new dimensions to academic cheating that require expanded frameworks for responsible human-AI collaboration.
methodology: Literature review and conceptual analysis of AI-enabled academic cheating and emerging anti-cheating approaches including facial recognition and watermarking
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how AI-generated academic content entering publication pipelines may contaminate web-scraped training corpora with non-authentic text.

key_claim: Adversarial training of AI-text detectors via the RADAR framework significantly improves robustness against LLM-based paraphrasing attacks.
methodology: Joint adversarial training framework (RADAR) that co-trains a paraphraser and a detector to improve robustness of AI-text detection
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether undetected AI-generated text accumulating in web corpora leads to information degradation and entropy decline in training data.

key_claim: Simple manipulation techniques reduce GenAI text detector accuracy by 17.4%, making them unreliable for determining academic integrity violations.
methodology: Comparative evaluation of six major GenAI text detectors on 805 modified machine-generated text samples using simple evasion techniques
limitation: Accuracy limitations and potential for false accusations undermine inclusive and fair assessment practices.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not explore how undetectable AI-generated text may increasingly populate web-scraped datasets, eroding diversity and authenticity of training signals.

key_claim: Semi-fragile watermarking via FaceSigns enables proactive media authentication that is more robust than post-hoc detection classifiers against black-box synthesis and adversarial examples.
methodology: Deep learning-based semi-fragile watermarking technique embedding invisible secret messages in image pixels for media provenance verification
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address watermarking or provenance verification for text-based web content, leaving text-domain training data authenticity unresolved.

key_claim: NLP-based methods can distinguish AI-generated phishing messages from both legitimate communication and human-written phishing attempts.
methodology: NLP classification approach trained on a balanced dataset of AI-generated phishing, human-written phishing, and legitimate communications
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider how AI-generated phishing text at scale contributes to the pollution of web-scraped corpora or the erosion of authentic human communication signals.

key_claim: Adaptively watermarking only high-entropy token distributions preserves text quality while maintaining robust, secure, and prompt-agnostic watermark detection.
methodology: Entropy-based adaptive watermarking strategy using an auxiliary model to selectively watermark high-entropy tokens while leaving low-entropy tokens untouched
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether watermark-free low-entropy tokens contribute to homogenization of web text or whether adaptive watermarking can help preserve training data diversity.

key_claim: LLM-prompted authorial impersonations using GPT-4o do not successfully evade existing forensic authorship verification systems across emails, text messages, and social media posts.
methodology: Generated impersonation texts using GPT-4o under four prompting conditions across three genres and evaluated against forensic authorship verification systems.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether LLM-generated impersonations introduce detectable statistical artifacts that differ from authentic human social behavioral patterns in training corpora.

key_claim: Augmenting reverse KL with forward KL when teacher entropy is high during on-policy distillation preserves generation diversity and stabilizes learning signals.
methodology: Entropy-aware on-policy distillation that dynamically blends reverse KL and forward KL objectives based on teacher distribution entropy.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether entropy-aware distillation can mitigate diversity loss originating from low-diversity or degraded web-scraped training data rather than from the distillation process itself.

key_claim: Symmetric alignment of intermediate layer thought processes via Logit Lens projections prevents both overconfidence and underconfidence in knowledge distillation of LLMs.
methodology: Projects intermediate hidden states into vocabulary space via the Logit Lens and enforces structural alignment using a symmetric divergence objective.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether distilled models preserve the capacity to represent rare or tail-distribution knowledge from training data during compression.
theoretical_framework: DistillLens (symmetric divergence alignment via Logit Lens)

key_claim: Standard RLVR objectives disproportionately reinforce highest-likelihood reasoning paths, causing mode collapse, which can be mitigated by an Advantage Re-weighting Mechanism incorporating prompt perplexity and answer confidence.
methodology: Analysis of sampling probability dynamics in GRPO, proposing Advantage Re-weighting Mechanism (ARM) to equilibrate confidence levels across correct responses.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether mode collapse in LLM reasoning is exacerbated by low-diversity or degraded web-scraped training data underlying the base model.

key_claim: Targeted unlearning via lightweight LoRA adapters can encode stealthy model provenance fingerprints that avoid high-perplexity triggers and fixed response patterns.
methodology: Builds key-value sets using an assistant model with predictive-entropy ranking, trains LoRA adapters to suppress original values on keys, and verifies ownership via likelihood and semantic evidence aggregation.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether provenance tracking methods can detect when models have been trained on degraded or synthetic web-scraped data versus authentic human-generated data.

key_claim: Composable semantic control codes derived from semantic representations enable flexible, targeted text perturbation without training separate models per perturbation type.
methodology: Builds on a pretrained seq2seq model conditioned on control codes from semantic representations, with composable operations to steer generation toward targeted attributes.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether semantically controlled perturbations can generate training data that preserves the authentic diversity and tail-distribution properties of real human text.

key_claim: A zero-shot multi-modal framework combining language perplexity scoring, autoencoder reconstruction, and semantic drift analysis effectively detects fake reviews without labeled training data.
methodology: Zero-shot framework integrating FLAN-T5 perplexity scoring, transformer-based autoencoder for structural deviations, semantic drift analysis, and device-level metadata from sensor-driven environments.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the detected anomaly patterns distinguish between LLM-generated fake reviews and degraded or low-quality authentic human reviews in web-scraped corpora.

key_claim: Information sufficiency and mutual information loss provide a formal characterization of the class of probabilistic models consistent with encoder-decoder latent predictive structures.
methodology: Information-theoretic analysis using information sufficiency (IS) and mutual information loss (MIL) to derive functional expressions characterizing encoder-decoder architectures.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether information loss in encoder-decoder compression disproportionately affects tail-distribution or rare signals from training data, contributing to diversity collapse.
theoretical_framework: Information Sufficiency (IS) and Mutual Information Loss (MIL) framework

key_claim: A reinforcement learning framework using proximal policy optimization can dynamically optimize watermark placement to balance detection reliability and linguistic fluency in neural text generation.
methodology: Policy network trained via proximal policy optimization for context-aware watermark placement decisions during text generation
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how watermarking interacts with degraded or low-diversity web-scraped training data or whether watermark robustness varies across authentic vs. synthetic text sources.
theoretical_framework: Reinforcement learning framework with proximal policy optimization for watermark placement

key_claim: A receiver-side In-Context Decoding framework enhances separate source-channel coding robustness against cliff effects in low SNR regimes without modifying the transmitter.
methodology: Error Correction Code Transformer (ECCT) providing bit-wise reliability estimates combined with confidence-ranked candidate construction for LLM-driven arithmetic coding
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider how the quality or diversity degradation of LLM training corpora affects the source coding model's entropy estimation and transmission robustness.
theoretical_framework: In-Context Decoding (ICD) framework for Separate Source-Channel Coding

key_claim: CCI4.0, a 35TB bilingual pre-training dataset with model-based quality scoring and domain-aware filtering, enhances reasoning capabilities in large language models.
methodology: Two-stage deduplication, multi-classifier quality scoring, and domain-aware fluency filtering pipeline applied to Chinese web corpus and English Nemotron-CC data
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not analyze whether the web-sourced data suffers from information degradation such as tail collapse or entropy decline, nor whether authentic human behavioral data could complement the corpus.

key_claim: A lightweight multitask model trained on LLM-annotated Romanian texts enables multi-level quality filtering that reveals noteworthy topic distribution differences between Romanian and English pretraining data.
methodology: LLM-annotated Romanian text used to train a multitask classifier for educational value, topic, and format filtering of pretraining corpora
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether under-represented language corpora suffer from amplified tail collapse or diversity loss compared to English web data.

key_claim: FineWeb, a 15-trillion token dataset from 96 Common Crawl snapshots with carefully ablated deduplication and filtering, produces better-performing LLMs than other open pretraining datasets.
methodology: Systematic ablation of deduplication and filtering strategies across 96 Common Crawl snapshots, including a classifier-based educational text filter for FineWeb-Edu
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not measure whether successive Common Crawl snapshots exhibit information degradation, entropy decline, or diversity loss over time.

key_claim: Yi model performance is primarily attributed to data quality from rigorous data-engineering efforts on a 3.1 trillion token corpus rather than architectural innovations.
methodology: Classical transformer architecture with scalable infrastructure; extensive data-engineering pipeline for curating 3.1 trillion token pretraining corpus; fine-tuning for chat, long-context, and vision-language variants
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the web-crawled training data exhibits tail collapse or diversity loss, nor whether physically-verified human behavioral data could provide irreplaceable training signals.

key_claim: Classifier-based quality filtering improves downstream task performance but does not necessarily enhance language modeling on the high-quality reference set because it implicitly filters the reference distribution as well.
methodology: In-depth analysis of classifier-based quality filtering (CQF) with comparisons to synthetic data training and controlled experiments on quality score distributions
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether CQF exacerbates tail collapse or diversity loss in web-scraped data by systematically removing underrepresented content.
theoretical_framework: Analytical framework decomposing classifier-based quality filtering behavior

key_claim: Conclusions about pretraining data quality from small proxy model experiments can flip with minor hyperparameter adjustments because optimal training configuration is inherently data-dependent.
methodology: Systematic variation of training hyperparameters across multiple data recipes at small scale to test transferability of proxy-model conclusions to full-scale training
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether proxy-model evaluations can detect subtle information degradation phenomena like tail collapse or entropy decline in web-scraped data.

key_claim: Synthetic multilingual pretraining data comprising 540B tokens across 10 Indic languages can be generated at scale using five different techniques with grounding in documents, personas, and topics.
methodology: Systematic generation of synthetic pretraining data using 5 techniques (document-grounded, persona-grounded, topic-grounded) for 10 Indic languages, with quality analysis across language prompts and grounding strategies
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether synthetic data for low-resource languages can replicate the cultural and social behavioral signals present in authentic human-generated text.

key_claim: Jointly optimizing data quality and diversity through a unified selection framework outperforms sequential approaches that first filter by quality then adjust proportions.
methodology: Unified data selection framework (QuaDMix) that automatically optimizes data distribution for LLM pretraining by jointly balancing quality and diversity metrics using multiple criteria
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether quality-diversity trade-offs change as web-scraped corpora degrade over time due to synthetic contamination and tail collapse.

key_claim: Sample-wise bottom-up data mixing that evaluates quality and diversity per sample outperforms domain-wise top-down approaches that ignore inter-domain overlaps.
methodology: Bottom-up sample-wise data mixing approach (SampleMix) that performs global cross-domain sampling by evaluating quality and diversity of each individual sample
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether sample-level diversity metrics can detect the entropy decline and tail collapse characteristic of degraded web-scraped corpora.

key_claim: Optimal strategies for large-scale dataset composition and filtering remain largely elusive, and transparency in data curation is essential for advancing open-source language models.
methodology: Release of RedPajama open dataset with artifacts and metadata addressing transparency, data quality, and reproducibility challenges in LLM pretraining
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether open web-scraped datasets like RedPajama exhibit information degradation over successive crawl snapshots or loss of authentic human behavioral signals.

key_claim: CLIP's success is primarily attributable to its data curation rather than model architecture, and metadata-based curation from a raw data pool can match or exceed CLIP's performance.
methodology: MetaCLIP: metadata-driven curation of a raw data pool using CLIP's concept vocabulary to produce a balanced subset, achieving competitive performance on standard vision benchmarks
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether metadata-based curation can preserve rare or tail-distributed human-generated content that is lost through aggressive balancing and filtering.

key_claim: Balanced pretraining data induces latent capabilities later activated during post-training, and SFT excels on smaller high-quality datasets while pretraining and RL require large-scale data.
methodology: Theoretical analysis of transformers trained on an in-context weight prediction task for linear regression to understand data quality requirements across pre- and post-training stages
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the theoretical framework accounts for information degradation in web-scraped pretraining data and the unique value of authentic human behavioral data.
theoretical_framework: In-context weight prediction task framework for linear regression analyzing transformer training dynamics

key_claim: Learning rate decay schedules undermine curriculum-based pretraining by applying the lowest learning rates to the highest-quality data presented last.
methodology: Controlled experiments comparing curriculum-based pretraining under constant vs. decaying learning rate schedules to isolate the interaction between data ordering and LR decay
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the scarcity of high-quality data driving curriculum approaches reflects broader information degradation in web-scraped corpora over time.

key_claim: Continual exposure to low-quality web text causes lasting cognitive decline in LLMs, including degraded reasoning, safety, and inflated dark personality traits, with dose-response effects.
methodology: Controlled continual pre-training experiments on 4 LLMs using real Twitter/X corpora with junk vs. control datasets constructed via engagement-degree (M1) and semantic-quality (M2) operationalizations, with matched token scale
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether authentic, physically-verified human social behavioral data can serve as an antidote or corrective signal to reverse the cognitive decline induced by junk web text.

key_claim: Incorporating reasoning-intensive data earlier during pretraining can improve LLM reasoning abilities compared to introducing it only during post-training.
methodology: Controlled experiments varying the phase (pretraining vs. post-training) at which reasoning data is introduced into LLM training pipelines
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the quality or authenticity of reasoning data degrades when sourced from web-scraped corpora versus human-verified behavioral data.

key_claim: BeyondWeb synthetic data outperforms state-of-the-art synthetic pretraining datasets like Cosmopedia and Nemotron-Synth by up to 5.1 percentage points.
methodology: Synthetic data generation framework (BeyondWeb) producing high-quality synthetic pretraining data evaluated against existing synthetic and web-scale datasets
limitation: Factors affecting synthetic data quality remain poorly understood despite empirical gains.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether synthetic data amplifies information degradation patterns (tail collapse, entropy decline) already present in the web data used as seeds.

key_claim: LLMs pretrained on just 21% of URLs selected by Craw4LLM achieve the same downstream performance as models trained on full previous crawls.
methodology: Web crawling guided by LLM pretraining influence as priority score, evaluated on a 900-million-webpage graph from a commercial search engine index
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether LLM-preference-based crawling further exacerbates tail collapse by systematically deprioritizing rare or diverse content.

key_claim: Organizing pretraining into four stages based on perplexity and perplexity difference achieves significant loss reductions four times during training.
methodology: Four-quadrant multi-stage pretraining strategy (FRAME) using quantitative PPL and PPL-difference criteria for data partitioning across stages
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider whether the perplexity-based data organization amplifies homogeneity by filtering out low-frequency authentic human behavioral signals.

key_claim: Incorporating knowledge gaps between dialogue participants in synthetic math conversations significantly improves LLM mathematical reasoning ability.
methodology: Generating synthetic dialogues (MIND) based on OpenWebMath with varied conversational settings including knowledge gaps between participants
limitation: Synthetic data typically fails to add complementary knowledge to the existing raw corpus for complex multi-hop reasoning.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not evaluate whether synthetic dialogue generation introduces systematic biases absent from authentic human mathematical discourse.

key_claim: Scalable data quality estimators like perplexity, Error L2-Norm, and memorization can systematically prune noisy web-scraped pretraining data to improve LLM performance.
methodology: Rigorous comparison at scale of perplexity, Error L2-Norm, and memorization metrics to rank and prune pretraining data from web scrapes
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether quality-based pruning disproportionately removes tail-distributed content representing diverse authentic human behaviors.

key_claim: Current LLM-driven synthetic data generation research lacks a unified framework and mostly remains surface-level.
methodology: Systematic organization of existing studies on LLM-driven synthetic data generation based on a generic workflow framework
limitation: Current investigations lack a unified framework and mostly stay on the surface.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not analyze whether LLM-generated synthetic data inherits and amplifies the entropy decline and diversity loss present in web-scraped training sources.
theoretical_framework: Generic workflow framework for synthetic data generation

key_claim: SELF-ALIGN enables LLM self-alignment using principle-driven reasoning with minimal human supervision, reducing reliance on costly human annotations.
methodology: Four-stage principle-driven self-alignment approach combining LLM generative power with human-defined principles, minimizing human supervision
limitation: Dependence on human supervision constrains AI potential due to high cost and issues with quality, reliability, diversity, self-consistency, and biases.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether self-alignment without authentic human behavioral feedback leads to value drift or loss of genuine social behavioral signals.

key_claim: Separating helpfulness and harmlessness annotations in human-preference datasets enables more effective safety alignment of large language models.
methodology: Collection of 333,963 safety meta-labels and 361,903 expert comparison pairs for QA pairs, applied to content moderation and RLHF.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether safety-aligned human preference data degrades in quality when sourced from web-scraped or synthetic interactions rather than authentic behavioral signals.

key_claim: RRHF aligns language models with human preferences using ranking loss on conditional probabilities, avoiding the instability and complexity of PPO-based RLHF.
methodology: Scoring sampled responses via log conditional probabilities and training with ranking loss to align with human preferences, compared against PPO-based RLHF.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the human preference signals used for alignment capture authentic behavioral diversity or are subject to the same biases as web-scraped training data.
theoretical_framework: RRHF (Rank Responses to Align with Human Feedback)

key_claim: Reinforcement learning is fundamentally a human-in-the-loop paradigm, and HITL approaches are particularly advantageous when reward functions are challenging to define.
methodology: Comprehensive literature survey and position paper on human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning requirements, challenges, and opportunities.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the quality and authenticity of human feedback signals degrade over time as humans increasingly interact with AI-generated content.

key_claim: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) can optimize language model policies directly from human preferences without fitting an explicit reward model or using reinforcement learning.
methodology: New parameterization of the reward model that enables direct extraction of the optimal policy in closed form, bypassing RL-based fine-tuning.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether human preference data used for alignment reflects authentic diverse human values or is biased by exposure to AI-generated text.

key_claim: RLAIF achieves comparable performance to RLHF across summarization and dialogue tasks, and can enable self-improvement even when the AI labeler matches the policy size.
methodology: Comparison of RLHF and RLAIF on summarization, helpful dialogue, and harmless dialogue tasks, including direct-RLAIF variant.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether replacing human feedback with AI-generated feedback accelerates information degradation and diversity loss in training signals.

key_claim: RLHF, at the intersection of AI and human-computer interaction, has been decisive in aligning large language models with human objectives.
methodology: Comprehensive literature survey covering RLHF methods, preference-based RL, and applications to LLMs.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether RLHF-derived alignment signals become less representative of authentic human preferences as training data increasingly contains model-generated content.

key_claim: Large-scale pre-training capturing knowledge across the web, combined with instruction fine-tuning and RLHF, are the key innovations driving LLM performance.
methodology: In-depth analysis of 194 arXiv papers on ChatGPT-related research including trend analysis, word cloud representation, and domain distribution analysis.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether web-scale pre-training data suffers from information degradation or tail collapse that limits LLM knowledge diversity.

key_claim: AI assistants fine-tuned with human feedback consistently exhibit sycophancy because human preference judgments favor responses matching user beliefs over truthful ones.
methodology: Evaluation of five state-of-the-art AI assistants on four free-form text-generation tasks, combined with analysis of existing human preference data for sycophancy bias.
what_it_does_NOT_address: How sycophantic model outputs fed back into web-scraped training corpora could amplify information degradation and reduce the diversity of authentic human viewpoints.
theoretical_framework: AI Supported Degradation of the Self Concept

key_claim: Distilled direct preference optimization using AI feedback produces a 7B chat model that achieves state-of-the-art alignment without human annotation.
methodology: Applied distilled supervised fine-tuning (dSFT) followed by distilled direct preference optimization (dDPO) using AI-generated preference data on a 7B parameter model.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether AI-generated preference feedback introduces homogenization or diversity loss compared to authentic human feedback signals.

key_claim: Current language models exhibit substantial misalignment with US demographic group opinions, on par with the Democrat-Republican divide on contentious topics.
methodology: Created OpinionsQA dataset leveraging high-quality public opinion polls to quantitatively compare LM-generated opinions against responses from 60 US demographic groups.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether opinion misalignment stems from training data composition biases such as overrepresentation of certain web-scraped demographics.
theoretical_framework: Quantitative framework for evaluating LM opinion alignment with demographic groups

key_claim: LLM-simulated human feedback is 50x cheaper than crowdworkers while displaying high agreement with real human annotators for instruction-following training.
methodology: Designed LLM prompts to simulate human feedback, proposed automatic evaluation validated against human judgments, and provided reference implementations for learning from feedback.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not assess whether replacing human feedback with LLM-simulated feedback degrades the diversity and authenticity of behavioral training signals over time.

key_claim: Process supervision providing feedback on each intermediate reasoning step significantly outperforms outcome supervision for training reliable mathematical reasoning models.
methodology: Compared process supervision versus outcome supervision for training models on challenging MATH benchmark problems, using human-annotated step-level feedback.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not explore whether process supervision signals could be sourced from authentic human problem-solving behavioral traces rather than explicit annotations.

key_claim: LLM self-reported personality traits are dissociated from their actual behavioral patterns, undermining the validity of questionnaire-based personality assessments of LLMs.
methodology: Systematically characterized LLM personality across training stages, validated self-reported traits against behavioral tasks, and tested persona injection interventions.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether the dissociation between self-reports and behavior arises from training data lacking authentic human behavioral data that grounds personality in action.

key_claim: Materials Cloud provides a comprehensive platform for sharing and reproducing computational science workflows through persistent, citable provenance graphs of entire simulation pipelines.
methodology: Built a platform integrating archival/dissemination services, modelling tools, data analytics, and AiiDA database sharing with full provenance tracking.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how curated scientific data platforms could serve as high-quality, physically-verified training data sources alternative to web-scraped corpora.

key_claim: RO-Crate provides a lightweight, community-driven approach to packaging research artefacts with machine-readable metadata using Schema.org annotations in JSON-LD.
methodology: Developed an open packaging format based on Schema.org JSON-LD annotations for structuring research artefact metadata with identifiers, provenance, and relations.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how structured provenance metadata could help assess or mitigate information degradation in web-scraped training corpora.

key_claim: Using large language models to generate search results reduces interaction and transparency, threatening information verification, provenance, and user trust.
methodology: Perspective paper revisiting search in the context of information seeking, arguing against reducing user interaction in favor of LLM-generated answers.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not empirically measure how LLM-generated search content may accelerate information degradation in web corpora through recursive synthetic content.

key_claim: AiiDA 1.0 achieves sustained throughputs of tens of thousands of processes per hour while automatically recording full data provenance for reproducible computational workflows.
methodology: Development of an open-source high-throughput workflow management infrastructure with automated provenance tracking, benchmarked on throughput performance
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how the quality or diversity of data managed by such computational infrastructures compares to physically-verified human behavioral data for training purposes.

key_claim: Despite advancements in privacy regulations and frameworks, privacy policies have remained persistently challenging to comprehend over two decades of evolution.
methodology: Sentence-level classifier applied to a large-scale longitudinal analysis of privacy policy documents, evaluating composition, readability, and structural evolution over time
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the degradation of privacy policy text quality over time may reflect broader information degradation trends in web-scraped corpora.

key_claim: Combining network theory with topic modelling into a temporal network of associated topics enables systematic evaluation of shifts and changes in latent content within unstructured text over time.
methodology: Temporal network analysis combined with topic modelling to identify non-stationary classes of co-occurring topics and evaluate trends in topic prevalence and association patterns
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether the temporal dynamics of latent topics reflect entropy decline or diversity loss in web-scraped training corpora.
theoretical_framework: Temporal network of associated topics (combining network theory and topic modelling)

key_claim: The Finnish Parliament ASR Corpus provides over 3000 hours of manually transcribed speech with rich demographic metadata and a natural longitudinal distribution-shift evaluation setting.
methodology: Collection and analysis of Finnish parliament meeting recordings and transcripts, with manual transcription correction and development of a complete Kaldi-based ASR pipeline
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether parliament speech data, as authentic human behavioral data, offers training signal advantages over web-scraped text for language models.

key_claim: Triangulated analysis of over 1.7 million Swiss news articles reveals distinct temporal dynamics in public discourse across French, German, and Italian linguistic regions.
methodology: Triangulated methodology combining lexical metrics, named entity recognition with Wikidata-based linking, targeted sentiment analysis, and consensus-based change-point detection on 1.7M+ news articles
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not assess whether multilingual news corpora exhibit information degradation or tail collapse that could affect their value as training data for language models.

key_claim: GPT-3 lacks social intelligence and Theory of Mind out-of-the-box, as demonstrated by poor performance on social reasoning tasks measuring understanding of intents and reactions.
methodology: Empirical evaluation of GPT-3 on SocialIQa and additional social reasoning tasks, combined with theory-based analysis of LLM social intelligence limitations
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether LLMs' Theory of Mind deficits stem from the absence of physically-verified authentic human social behavioral data in web-scraped training corpora.

key_claim: Existing ToM benchmarks for LLMs are limited by text-only inputs and narrow focus on belief tasks; CoMMET provides a multimodal, multi-turn benchmark covering a broader range of mental states.
methodology: Construction of a multimodal benchmark dataset inspired by the Theory of Mind Booklet Task, with expanded mental state categories and multi-turn testing of LLMs
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not explore whether LLMs' social reasoning deficiencies are attributable to the lack of authentic human social behavioral signals in their web-scraped training data.

key_claim: Reinforcement learning on adversarially hard social reasoning examples improves LLM social intelligence beyond reliance on superficial patterns.
methodology: Introduction of ToMBench-Hard adversarial benchmark and Social-R1 reinforcement learning framework to train LLMs on challenging social reasoning cases that resist shortcut solutions
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether incorporating physically-verified human social behavioral data, rather than synthetic adversarial examples, could more fundamentally address LLMs' social reasoning deficits.

key_claim: Existing evaluations fail to assess agents' ability to integrate physical and social constraints, and the S3IT benchmark addresses this gap via a seat-ordering task in 3D environments.
methodology: Construction of a benchmark centered on a seat-ordering task requiring agents to reason over social norms and physical constraints in a 3D environment
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether embodied social intelligence benchmarks require grounding in authentic human behavioral data rather than synthetically designed scenarios.

key_claim: Frontier VLMs achieve over 93% accuracy on cultural Theory of Mind tasks but still struggle with false belief reasoning (19-83%) and show 20-30% regional performance variance.
methodology: Construction of CulturalToM-VQA benchmark (5,095 probes) via frontier MLLM generation with human verification, benchmarking 10 VLMs across six ToM tasks and four complexity levels
limitation: Models struggle with false belief reasoning and show high regional variance (20-30% gap).
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether cross-cultural ToM failures stem from biased or culturally homogeneous web-scraped training data rather than architectural limitations.
theoretical_framework: Theory of Mind taxonomy with six tasks and four complexity levels

key_claim: MetaMind, a metacognitive multi-agent framework decomposing social reasoning into Theory-of-Mind, Moral, and Response agents, emulates human-like social reasoning better than standard LLMs.
methodology: Multi-agent framework with three collaborative stages: ToM Agent for mental state hypotheses, Moral Agent for cultural/ethical refinement, and Response Agent for output generation
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether metacognitive multi-agent reasoning can compensate for the absence of authentic human social behavioral data in training corpora.
theoretical_framework: Metacognitive multi-agent framework inspired by psychological theories of metacognition and Theory of Mind

key_claim: Current AI social reasoning relies on language-dominant approaches, causing systems to struggle with nonverbal social understanding, which mime videos can uniquely address.
methodology: Construction of the MimeQA dataset from mime videos as a novel nonverbal social interaction data source for benchmarking and training models
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether nonverbal social signals from mime performances generalize to authentic, naturally occurring human behavioral data in everyday social interactions.

key_claim: In multi-speaker scenes, MLLMs exhibit weak cross-modal attention alignment between visual and textual tokens, which a proposed multi-speaker attention alignment method can correct.
methodology: Quantitative analysis of cross-modal attention in state-of-the-art MLLMs followed by a dynamic cross-modal head selection method for speaker-consistent alignment
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the cross-modal attention misalignment in multi-speaker social scenes is partly caused by insufficient authentic multi-party social interaction data in training corpora.

key_claim: LLMs' apparent social reasoning abilities may reflect superficial pattern matching (Clever Hans effect) rather than genuine neural Theory of Mind.
methodology: Stress testing social reasoning capabilities of large language models through controlled evaluations
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether LLMs' shallow social reasoning is attributable to degraded or homogeneous social behavioral signals in web-scraped training data.

key_claim: Human-like easy-to-difficult curriculum learning during finetuning improves language model performance on commonsense reasoning tasks compared to random data ordering.
methodology: Paced curriculum learning with static and adaptive difficulty ranking during finetuning of pretrained language models on commonsense reasoning tasks
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether curriculum learning can mitigate information degradation or diversity loss in the underlying training data used for commonsense reasoning.

key_claim: A two-stage generative prompting framework leveraging implicit knowledge in pretrained language models outperforms retrieval-based methods for unsupervised commonsense QA.
methodology: Two-stage prompt-based framework (TSGP): knowledge generation prompts produce unlimited-type knowledge, then answer generation prompts select answers without labeled task data
limitation: Previous methods retrieved from traditional knowledge bases or generated fixed types of knowledge with poor generalization ability.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the implicit commonsense knowledge in pretrained language models degrades as web-scraped training data becomes less diverse over time.

key_claim: A unified commonsense reasoning model (UNICORN) trained on the Rainbow multitask benchmark generalizes well across diverse commonsense tasks and datasets.
methodology: Multitask fine-tuning of pretrained language models across multiple commonsense benchmarks with a novel cost equivalent curve evaluation over 4800 models
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether commonsense reasoning benchmarks suffer from data homogeneity or tail collapse due to web-scraped training sources.

key_claim: Pre-trained text-conditioned image editing models can be adapted for multiple image restoration tasks using only 16-128 paired examples via LoRA fine-tuning.
methodology: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning of LoRA adapters on FLUX.1 Kontext (12B parameter flow matching model) with 16-128 paired images per restoration task
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider whether the visual priors in pre-trained editing models degrade when trained on web-scraped image data of declining diversity.

key_claim: Decomposing agent capabilities into reasoner, executor, and summarizer roles with a Mixture-of-Roles architecture enables effective parameter-efficient fine-tuning for agent tasks.
methodology: Mixture-of-Roles (MoR) parameter-efficient fine-tuning decomposing agent tasks into three role-specific adapters
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether agent task performance is affected by training data quality degradation or loss of diverse behavioral signals in web-scraped corpora.
theoretical_framework: Mixture-of-Roles (MoR) framework based on Reason+Action paradigm

key_claim: LoRA fine-tuning can unintentionally compromise safety alignment in LLM agents, and spectral sharpness-guided pruning of LoRA updates can mitigate safety risks without requiring base model checkpoints.
methodology: Magnitude-Aware Spherically Normalized SVD (MAS-SVD) for data-free, model-independent pruning of LoRA weight updates to preserve safety alignment
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the quality or diversity of the fine-tuning data itself contributes to safety degradation versus the adaptation mechanism.
theoretical_framework: S3LoRA (Safe Spectral Sharpness-Guided Pruning LoRA) framework

key_claim: Data-driven closed-form initialization of LoRA weights using domain shift constraints improves convergence and final performance over random initialization.
methodology: Formulating LoRA initialization as a domain shift problem with constraints relating pre-training and fine-tuning activations to derive closed-form weight estimates
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether pre-training data degradation or entropy decline affects the quality of the activation-based initialization signals.

key_claim: LoRA fine-tuning of Llama3 with fishery-specific datasets effectively adapts the model for intelligent fisheries management while reducing computational cost.
methodology: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) fine-tuning of Llama3 model on increased fishery domain datasets
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider whether the domain-specific fishery data provides irreplaceable authentic signals that web-scraped general corpora cannot capture.

key_claim: Integrating LoRA fine-tuning with GraphRAG significantly improves question answering accuracy for the ancient ceramics domain compared to base foundation models.
methodology: LoRA fine-tuning of Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct on 2143 manually curated QA pairs generated via GLM-4V-9B and ERNIE 4.0 Turbo, combined with Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether reliance on LLM-generated QA pairs introduces synthetic data feedback loops that reduce information diversity compared to authentic human expert annotations.

key_claim: D2-LoRA improves average accuracy by 2.2 percentage points over LoRA across eight QA benchmarks using only 5k training samples per task while preserving inference-time mergeability.
methodology: Signed low-rank residual updates with additive/subtractive components and column-wise projection, merged into single weight matrix post-training, evaluated on eight QA/reading comprehension benchmarks
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate how the quality or diversity of the small-scale fine-tuning data interacts with the adaptation method's effectiveness under data degradation scenarios.
dataset_or_benchmark: eight question answering and reading comprehension benchmarks

key_claim: C2C-MoLA achieves cross-type chart-to-code generalization with memory efficiency by combining Mixture of Experts with Low-Rank Adaptation and complexity-aware routing.
methodology: Multimodal framework combining MoE with complexity-aware routing mechanism and LoRA for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of chart-to-code generation
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the training data used for chart-to-code generation suffers from diversity loss or representational biases inherent in web-scraped chart corpora.
theoretical_framework: Mixture of Experts with Low-Rank Adaptation (MoE + LoRA) framework with complexity-aware routing
